A rider's availability/lifecycle status changed.
emitted by: 🎭 Rider
consumed by: —
projected into: —
FieldTypeReq.Description
riderId🔤 RiderId✅ required
status🔤 RiderStatus✅ required
🔤 Scalars (9)
ScalarTypeDescription
🔤 DeliveryJobIdstring uuidIdentifies one DeliveryJob (a single delivery of an order from restaurant to customer).
🔤 RiderIdstring uuidAn independent Captain rider (courier). Null on a partner-fulfilled job (the partner's courier is name/phone only).
🔤 CityIdstring uuidA city Captain operates in — the scope that anchors delivery routing config (CityDeliveryRanking) and, later, partner availability. First-class id (delivery dispatch strategy foundation, #60).
🔤 RiderStatusenum (OFFLINE | AVAILABLE | ON_DELIVERY | SUSPENDED)Availability/lifecycle status of an independent Captain rider.
🔤 DeliveryProviderenum (PARTNER | INDEPENDENT)Fulfilment channel of a delivery: PARTNER (e.g. Avelo37) or INDEPENDENT (a Captain rider).
🔤 DeliveryChannelKeystring ^[a-z0-9]+(?:_[a-z0-9]+)*$Slug key of a delivery channel in the DeliveryChannelCatalog (e.g. 'independent', 'avelo37', 'uber_direct', 'coopcycle'). Data-driven (a new partner = a catalog row + an adapter), so channels are NOT a fixed enum (#60).
🔤 DeliveryPartnerRegistrationIdstring uuidA delivery partner's self-registration of availability to serve one city on one catalog channel (#61). Client-generated; the aggregate id of the DeliveryPartnerRegistration.
🔤 DeliveryPartnerNamestringThe delivery partner's display/legal name as stated on self-registration (#61).
🔤 CityAvailabilityStatusenum (PENDING | APPROVED | REVOKED)Review state of a delivery partner's declared availability to serve a city (#61): PENDING until an admin approves, APPROVED = live for dispatch consideration, REVOKED = withdrawn/disabled.
⛔ Errors (9)
ErrorDescriptionMessage (en)Message (fr)Thrown by
⛔ DeliveryJobNotFoundNo delivery job with this id.🇬🇧 Delivery job not found.🇫🇷 Livraison introuvable.📩 AcceptDelivery, 📩 ConfirmPickup, 📩 CompleteDelivery, 📩 CancelDelivery, 📩 EscalateDelivery, 📩 ReportDeliveryIssue, 📩 ResolveDeliveryIssue, 📩 UpdateDeliveryStatus, 📩 AssignDeliveryToPartner, 📩 UnassignDeliveryFromPartner, 📩 UpdateDeliveryPartnerStatus, 📩 DeclineDelivery
⛔ InvalidDeliveryStatusThe delivery job is not in a status that allows this transition.🇬🇧 This action is not allowed while the delivery is '{currentStatus}'.🇫🇷 Cette action n'est pas autorisée tant que la livraison est '{currentStatus}'.📩 AcceptDelivery, 📩 ConfirmPickup, 📩 CompleteDelivery, 📩 CancelDelivery, 📩 ReportDeliveryIssue, 📩 ResolveDeliveryIssue, 📩 UpdateDeliveryStatus, 📩 AssignDeliveryToPartner, 📩 UnassignDeliveryFromPartner, 📩 UpdateDeliveryPartnerStatus, 📩 DeclineDelivery
⛔ DeliveryAlreadyAssignedThe delivery job has already been accepted by a courier/rider.🇬🇧 This delivery has already been taken.🇫🇷 Cette livraison a déjà été prise en charge.📩 AcceptDelivery, 📩 AssignDeliveryToPartner, 📩 DeclineDelivery
⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityAlreadyRequestedA registration with this id already exists (idempotent self-registration guard).🇬🇧 This availability registration already exists.🇫🇷 Cette demande de disponibilité existe déjà.📩 RegisterDeliveryPartnerAvailability
⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityNotFoundNo delivery-partner availability registration with this id.🇬🇧 Availability registration not found.🇫🇷 Demande de disponibilité introuvable.📩 ApproveDeliveryPartnerAvailability, 📩 RevokeDeliveryPartnerAvailability
⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityNotPendingThe availability registration is not PENDING, so it cannot be approved.🇬🇧 This availability registration is not awaiting review.🇫🇷 Cette demande de disponibilité n'est pas en attente de validation.📩 ApproveDeliveryPartnerAvailability
⛔ RiderAlreadyRegisteredA rider is already registered for this identity (authRef/id).🇬🇧 You are already registered as a rider.🇫🇷 Vous êtes déjà inscrit en tant que livreur.📩 RegisterRider
⛔ RiderNotFoundNo rider with this id.🇬🇧 Rider not found.🇫🇷 Livreur introuvable.📩 UpdateRiderInfo, 📩 ChangeRiderStatus
⛔ InvalidRiderStatusTransitionThe rider is not in a status that allows this transition.🇬🇧 A rider cannot move from '{currentStatus}' to '{targetStatus}'.🇫🇷 Un livreur ne peut pas passer de '{currentStatus}' à '{targetStatus}'.📩 ChangeRiderStatus
📐 Business rules (18)
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryAcceptedOnlyWhenPending🔗 #rule-deliveryacceptedonlywhenpending
A pending delivery job can be accepted by an independent rider only once.
verified by: 🧪 TestAcceptDelivery, 🧪 TestAcceptDeliveryIsRejected
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryPickupAndCompletionByRider🔗 #rule-deliverypickupandcompletionbyrider
The assigned rider confirms pickup then records hand-over, in the correct order.
verified by: 🧪 TestConfirmPickup, 🧪 TestCompleteDelivery, 🧪 TestDeliveryStatusUpdatedByCommand
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryCancellableBeforeCompletion🔗 #rule-deliverycancellablebeforecompletion
A delivery job can be cancelled before completion but not after it is delivered.
verified by: 🧪 TestCancelDelivery, 🧪 TestCancelDeliveryIsRejected
▸Rule: 📐 ReadyDeliveryOrderTriggersDispatch🔗 #rule-readydeliveryordertriggersdispatch
A ready DELIVERY order triggers creation of a delivery job (dispatch).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchOnOrderReady, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobBornOnDeliveryRequested
▸Rule: 📐 PartnerAcceptanceRecordsCourier🔗 #rule-partneracceptancerecordscourier
When the partner accepts (inbound), the assigned courier is recorded on the job.
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchPartnerAccepted, 🧪 TestDispatchAcceptedAfterReoffer, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerAcceptance
▸Rule: 📐 PartnerRejectionReoffers🔗 #rule-partnerrejectionreoffers
When the partner declines (inbound), the job is re-offered or flagged for manual handling.
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchPartnerRejected, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerRejection
▸Rule: 📐 DispatchExhaustionFailsClosed🔗 #rule-dispatchexhaustionfailsclosed
When Captain dispatches, the delivery job walks the city's ranked channel list, offering each ranked channel at most once in rank order; once every ranked channel has been offered and none accepts, the dispatch fails terminally (DeliveryDispatchFailed, run FAILED) and is surfaced to the restaurant for manual handling — never an unbounded retry loop (#60, supersedes ADR-20260720-004556).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchFailsAfterOfferCap, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsDispatchFailure
▸Rule: 📐 RestaurantDispatchBypassesRouting🔗 #rule-restaurantdispatchbypassesrouting
A RESTAURANT-dispatch order (RestaurantDispatchMode RESTAURANT) is never offered to a delivery channel: the dispatch run short-circuits to SELF_DISPATCHED and Captain only tracks the delivery, while the restaurant drives its progress and the customer still gets order tracking (#60).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchSelfDispatched
▸Rule: 📐 CityRankingWalkedInOrder🔗 #rule-cityrankingwalkedinorder
When Captain dispatches, channels are offered in the city's configured rank order (CityDeliveryRanking, rank 1 first): the birth offer goes to the rank-1 channel and each advance moves to the next rank (#60).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchPartnerRejected, 🧪 TestDispatchAcceptedAfterReoffer
▸Rule: 📐 TimeoutEscalatesToNextChannel🔗 #rule-timeoutescalatestonextchannel
When an outstanding delivery offer times out (DeliveryOfferTimedOut), the dispatch advances to the next ranked channel (or fails closed when the ranked walk is exhausted) — a stale offer never blocks the walk (#60).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchAdvancesOnTimeout, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsOfferTimeout
▸Rule: 📐 ManualEscalateSkipsChannel🔗 #rule-manualescalateskipschannel
An operator (restaurant/admin) may escalate an outstanding delivery offer (EscalateDelivery → DeliveryEscalationRequested), skipping the channel currently offered and advancing to the next ranked channel (or failing closed when the ranked walk is exhausted) (#60).
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchAdvancesOnEscalate, 🧪 TestDeliveryJobEscalated
▸Rule: 📐 OrderClosedOnDeliveryCompletion🔗 #rule-orderclosedondeliverycompletion
The order is closed (OrderDelivered) when the partner reports DELIVERED or an independent rider completes the delivery.
verified by: 🧪 TestDispatchClosesOrderOnPartnerDelivered, 🧪 TestDispatchClosesOrderOnRiderCompleted
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAssignmentLifecycle🔗 #rule-deliverypartnerassignmentlifecycle
A PENDING delivery job can be assigned to a delivery partner (once), unassigned to be re-offered, and partner-reported status changes apply only as valid transitions.
verified by: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerStatusReport, 🧪 TestDeliveryAssignedToPartner, 🧪 TestDeliveryUnassignedFromPartner, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerStatusUpdated
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryDeclineKeepsJobPending🔗 #rule-deliverydeclinekeepsjobpending
An independent rider may decline a pending delivery job; the job stays PENDING and re-offerable.
verified by: 🧪 TestDeliveryDeclinedByRider
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryIssueLifecycle🔗 #rule-deliveryissuelifecycle
A delivery issue can be reported on a non-delivered job and later resolved.
verified by: 🧪 TestDeliveryIssueReported, 🧪 TestDeliveryIssueResolved
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryPartnerSelfRegistersCityAvailability🔗 #rule-deliverypartnerselfregisterscityavailability
A delivery partner can self-register (via the EXTERNAL API) its availability to serve a city on a catalog channel; the registration is idempotent by its client-generated id and lands PENDING (#61).
verified by: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerRegisterAgainIsRejected
▸Rule: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval🔗 #rule-deliverypartneravailabilitygoesliveonlyafterapproval
A self-registered city availability becomes APPROVED (eligible for the city's dispatch ranking) only after an admin approves a PENDING registration; approving anything not PENDING is rejected, and a registration may later be revoked by the partner or an admin (#61).
verified by: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityApproved, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerApproveMissingIsRejected, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerApproveNonPendingIsRejected, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRevoked, 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerRevokeMissingIsRejected
▸Rule: 📐 RiderLifecycle🔗 #rule-riderlifecycle
A rider registers once (per auth user), may update editable profile fields, and changes availability status only through valid transitions.
verified by: 🧪 TestRiderRegistered, 🧪 TestRiderRegisterAgainIsRejected, 🧪 TestRiderInfoUpdated, 🧪 TestRiderUpdateIsRejectedWhenNotFound, 🧪 TestRiderStatusChanged, 🧪 TestRiderStatusChangeIsRejected
🧪 Tests (4)

🎭 DeliveryPartnerRegistration

▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested🔗 #test-testdeliverypartneravailabilityrequested
A delivery partner self-registers availability to serve a city on a catalog channel
given: (none)
when: 📩 RegisterDeliveryPartnerAvailability
then: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerSelfRegistersCityAvailability
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerRegisterAgainIsRejected🔗 #test-testdeliverypartnerregisteragainisrejected
Re-registering the same availability id is rejected (idempotent birth)
given: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested
when: 📩 RegisterDeliveryPartnerAvailability
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityAlreadyRequested
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerSelfRegistersCityAvailability
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityApproved🔗 #test-testdeliverypartneravailabilityapproved
An admin approves a pending city-availability registration
given: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested
when: 📩 ApproveDeliveryPartnerAvailability
then: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityApproved
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerApproveMissingIsRejected🔗 #test-testdeliverypartnerapprovemissingisrejected
Approving an unknown availability registration is rejected
given: (none)
when: 📩 ApproveDeliveryPartnerAvailability
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityNotFound
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerApproveNonPendingIsRejected🔗 #test-testdeliverypartnerapprovenonpendingisrejected
Approving an already-approved registration is rejected (not pending)
given: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested, ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityApproved
when: 📩 ApproveDeliveryPartnerAvailability
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityNotPending
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRevoked🔗 #test-testdeliverypartneravailabilityrevoked
A partner (or admin) revokes a city-availability registration
given: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRequested
when: 📩 RevokeDeliveryPartnerAvailability
then: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityRevoked
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerRevokeMissingIsRejected🔗 #test-testdeliverypartnerrevokemissingisrejected
Revoking an unknown availability registration is rejected
given: (none)
when: 📩 RevokeDeliveryPartnerAvailability
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityNotFound
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAvailabilityGoesLiveOnlyAfterApproval

🎭 DeliveryJob

▸Test: 🧪 TestAcceptDelivery🔗 #test-testacceptdelivery
An independent rider accepts a pending delivery job
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 AcceptDelivery
then: ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider
verifies: 📐 DeliveryAcceptedOnlyWhenPending
▸Test: 🧪 TestAcceptDeliveryIsRejected🔗 #test-testacceptdeliveryisrejected
Rejects accepting a missing job or one already taken
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider
when: 📩 AcceptDelivery
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryJobNotFound, ⛔ InvalidDeliveryStatus, ⛔ DeliveryAlreadyAssigned
verifies: 📐 DeliveryAcceptedOnlyWhenPending
▸Test: 🧪 TestConfirmPickup🔗 #test-testconfirmpickup
The assigned rider confirms pickup from the restaurant
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider
when: 📩 ConfirmPickup
then: ⚡ DeliveryPickedUp
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPickupAndCompletionByRider
▸Test: 🧪 TestCompleteDelivery🔗 #test-testcompletedelivery
The assigned rider records hand-over to the customer
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider, ⚡ DeliveryPickedUp
when: 📩 CompleteDelivery
then: ⚡ DeliveryCompleted
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPickupAndCompletionByRider
▸Test: 🧪 TestCancelDelivery🔗 #test-testcanceldelivery
The restaurant cancels a pending delivery job
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 CancelDelivery
then: ⚡ DeliveryCancelled
verifies: 📐 DeliveryCancellableBeforeCompletion
▸Test: 🧪 TestCancelDeliveryIsRejected🔗 #test-testcanceldeliveryisrejected
Rejects cancelling a missing or already-delivered job
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider, ⚡ DeliveryPickedUp, ⚡ DeliveryCompleted
when: 📩 CancelDelivery
thrown: ⛔ DeliveryJobNotFound, ⛔ InvalidDeliveryStatus
verifies: 📐 DeliveryCancellableBeforeCompletion
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobBornOnDeliveryRequested🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobbornondeliveryrequested
The DeliveryJob is born by recording the DeliveryRequested fact delivered by DeliveryDispatchProcess (idempotent)
given: (none)
when: 📩 DeliveryRequested
then: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
verifies: 📐 ReadyDeliveryOrderTriggersDispatch
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerAcceptance🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobrecordspartneracceptance
The DeliveryJob records the inbound partner acceptance with its courier (idempotent by partnerRef)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
then: ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
verifies: 📐 PartnerAcceptanceRecordsCourier
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerRejection🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobrecordspartnerrejection
The DeliveryJob records the inbound partner rejection (the dispatcher re-offers, bounded)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryRejectedByPartner
then: ⚡ DeliveryRejectedByPartner
verifies: 📐 PartnerRejectionReoffers
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsDispatchFailure🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobrecordsdispatchfailure
The DeliveryJob records the terminal dispatch failure delivered by DeliveryDispatchProcess (job → FAILED)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryDispatchFailed
then: ⚡ DeliveryDispatchFailed
verifies: 📐 DispatchExhaustionFailsClosed
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobEscalated🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobescalated
An operator escalates an outstanding offer: the DeliveryJob emits DeliveryEscalationRequested
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 EscalateDelivery
then: ⚡ DeliveryEscalationRequested
verifies: 📐 ManualEscalateSkipsChannel
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsOfferTimeout🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobrecordsoffertimeout
The DeliveryJob records the offer-timeout fact (the dispatcher advances the ranked walk)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryOfferTimedOut
then: ⚡ DeliveryOfferTimedOut
verifies: 📐 TimeoutEscalatesToNextChannel
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryJobRecordsPartnerStatusReport🔗 #test-testdeliveryjobrecordspartnerstatusreport
The DeliveryJob records the inbound partner status report as a valid transition (avelo37 inbox, issue #28)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
when: 📩 DeliveryStatusUpdated
then: ⚡ DeliveryStatusUpdated
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAssignmentLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryStatusUpdatedByCommand🔗 #test-testdeliverystatusupdatedbycommand
The delivery status is driven through valid transitions to DELIVERED
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider, ⚡ DeliveryPickedUp
when: 📩 UpdateDeliveryStatus
then: ⚡ DeliveryStatusUpdated
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPickupAndCompletionByRider
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryAssignedToPartner🔗 #test-testdeliveryassignedtopartner
A pending delivery job is assigned to a delivery partner
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 AssignDeliveryToPartner
then: ⚡ DeliveryAssignedToPartner
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAssignmentLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryUnassignedFromPartner🔗 #test-testdeliveryunassignedfrompartner
An assigned delivery job is unassigned from its partner to be re-offered
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAssignedToPartner
when: 📩 UnassignDeliveryFromPartner
then: ⚡ DeliveryUnassignedFromPartner
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAssignmentLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryPartnerStatusUpdated🔗 #test-testdeliverypartnerstatusupdated
A partner-reported status change applies to the job as a valid transition
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
when: 📩 UpdateDeliveryPartnerStatus
then: ⚡ DeliveryPartnerStatusUpdated
verifies: 📐 DeliveryPartnerAssignmentLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryDeclinedByRider🔗 #test-testdeliverydeclinedbyrider
An independent rider declines a pending job; it stays PENDING and re-offerable
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeclineDelivery
then: ⚡ DeliveryDeclinedByRider
verifies: 📐 DeliveryDeclineKeepsJobPending
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryIssueReported🔗 #test-testdeliveryissuereported
An issue is reported on a non-delivered job
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider
when: 📩 ReportDeliveryIssue
then: ⚡ DeliveryIssueReported
verifies: 📐 DeliveryIssueLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestDeliveryIssueResolved🔗 #test-testdeliveryissueresolved
A previously reported delivery issue is resolved
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider, ⚡ DeliveryIssueReported
when: 📩 ResolveDeliveryIssue
then: ⚡ DeliveryIssueResolved
verifies: 📐 DeliveryIssueLifecycle

🎭 Rider

▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderRegistered🔗 #test-testriderregistered
A rider registers (linked to the auth provider user)
given: (none)
when: 📩 RegisterRider
then: ⚡ RiderRegistered
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderRegisterAgainIsRejected🔗 #test-testriderregisteragainisrejected
Registering the same rider twice is rejected
given: ⚡ RiderRegistered
when: 📩 RegisterRider
thrown: ⛔ RiderAlreadyRegistered
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderInfoUpdated🔗 #test-testriderinfoupdated
A rider updates editable profile fields
given: ⚡ RiderRegistered
when: 📩 UpdateRiderInfo
then: ⚡ RiderInfoUpdated
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderUpdateIsRejectedWhenNotFound🔗 #test-testriderupdateisrejectedwhennotfound
Updating an unknown rider is rejected
given: (none)
when: 📩 UpdateRiderInfo
thrown: ⛔ RiderNotFound
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderStatusChanged🔗 #test-testriderstatuschanged
A rider goes AVAILABLE (valid transition from OFFLINE)
given: ⚡ RiderRegistered
when: 📩 ChangeRiderStatus
then: ⚡ RiderStatusChanged
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle
▸Test: 🧪 TestRiderStatusChangeIsRejected🔗 #test-testriderstatuschangeisrejected
An OFFLINE rider cannot jump straight to ON_DELIVERY (invalid transition)
given: ⚡ RiderRegistered
when: 📩 ChangeRiderStatus
thrown: ⛔ InvalidRiderStatusTransition
verifies: 📐 RiderLifecycle

🎭 DeliveryDispatchProcess

▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchOnOrderReady🔗 #test-testdispatchonorderready
A ready DELIVERY order triggers creation of a delivery job
given: ⚡ RestaurantRegistered, ⚡ OrderPlaced
when: 📩 OrderMarkedReady
then: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
verifies: 📐 ReadyDeliveryOrderTriggersDispatch
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchPartnerAccepted🔗 #test-testdispatchpartneraccepted
Records the assigned courier when the partner accepts (inbound)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
then: ∅ no event (idempotent no-op)
verifies: 📐 PartnerAcceptanceRecordsCourier
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchPartnerRejected🔗 #test-testdispatchpartnerrejected
Advances the ranked channel walk to the next channel on a partner decline (inbound)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryRejectedByPartner
then: ∅ no event (idempotent no-op)
verifies: 📐 PartnerRejectionReoffers, 📐 CityRankingWalkedInOrder
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchAcceptedAfterReoffer🔗 #test-testdispatchacceptedafterreoffer
A channel accepts a re-offered job after the walk advanced: the run reaches ACCEPTED
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryRejectedByPartner
when: 📩 DeliveryAcceptedByPartner
then: ∅ no event (idempotent no-op)
verifies: 📐 CityRankingWalkedInOrder, 📐 PartnerAcceptanceRecordsCourier
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchFailsAfterOfferCap🔗 #test-testdispatchfailsafteroffercap
Exhausting the ranked channel walk fails the dispatch closed (DeliveryDispatchFailed, run FAILED)
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryRejectedByPartner, ⚡ DeliveryRejectedByPartner
when: 📩 DeliveryRejectedByPartner
then: ⚡ DeliveryDispatchFailed
verifies: 📐 DispatchExhaustionFailsClosed
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchSelfDispatched🔗 #test-testdispatchselfdispatched
A RESTAURANT-dispatch order is not offered to a channel: the run self-dispatches (Captain tracks only)
given: ⚡ RestaurantRegistered, ⚡ OrderPlaced
when: 📩 OrderMarkedReady
then: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
verifies: 📐 RestaurantDispatchBypassesRouting
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchAdvancesOnEscalate🔗 #test-testdispatchadvancesonescalate
A manual operator escalate skips the current channel and advances the ranked walk
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryEscalationRequested
then: ∅ no event (idempotent no-op)
verifies: 📐 ManualEscalateSkipsChannel
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchAdvancesOnTimeout🔗 #test-testdispatchadvancesontimeout
An expired offer advances the ranked walk to the next channel
given: ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryOfferTimedOut
then: ∅ no event (idempotent no-op)
verifies: 📐 TimeoutEscalatesToNextChannel
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchClosesOrderOnPartnerDelivered🔗 #test-testdispatchclosesorderonpartnerdelivered
Closes the order when the partner reports DELIVERED (inbound)
given: ⚡ OrderPlaced, ⚡ OrderAcceptedByRestaurant, ⚡ OrderMarkedReady, ⚡ DeliveryRequested
when: 📩 DeliveryStatusUpdated
then: ⚡ OrderDelivered
verifies: 📐 OrderClosedOnDeliveryCompletion
▸Test: 🧪 TestDispatchClosesOrderOnRiderCompleted🔗 #test-testdispatchclosesorderonridercompleted
Closes the order when an independent rider completes the delivery
given: ⚡ OrderPlaced, ⚡ OrderAcceptedByRestaurant, ⚡ OrderMarkedReady, ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryAcceptedByRider, ⚡ DeliveryPickedUp
when: 📩 DeliveryCompleted
then: ⚡ OrderDelivered
verifies: 📐 OrderClosedOnDeliveryCompletion
📡 Observability (1)
▸Contract: 📡 delivery-dispatch-strategy🔗 #obs-delivery-dispatch-strategy
criticality: high
workflow: saga 📦 DeliveryDispatchProcess
emits: ⚡ DeliveryRequested, ⚡ DeliveryDispatchFailed · inbound: ⚡ OrderMarkedReady, ⚡ DeliveryRejectedByPartner, ⚡ DeliveryEscalationRequested, ⚡ DeliveryOfferTimedOut
run identity
IdSourceReq.Business key
correlation_idevent.correlation_id✅—
trace_idotel.trace_id✅—
order_iddomain.aggregate_id✅🔤 OrderId
delivery_job_iddomain.delivery_job_id✅🔤 DeliveryJobId
spans (* = required attribute)
SpanKindReq.MultiplicityAttributes
event.consume.triggerCONSUMER✅—business.event_type*
dispatch.resolve_strategyINTERNAL✅—business.dispatch_mode*, business.city_id
dispatch.select_channelINTERNAL⬜—business.channel*, business.rank*, business.channels_remaining*
delivery.offer_jobCLIENT⬜—business.channel*, business.result*
event.store.appendINTERNAL✅—business.event_type*, business.stream_id*
metrics: delivery_dispatch_duration_ms (histogram), delivery_offer_timeout_ms (histogram) · business: delivery_offers_total (counter), delivery_dispatch_failed_total (counter), delivery_self_dispatched_total (counter)
success ⇐ spans: event.consume.trigger, dispatch.resolve_strategy, event.store.append
SLOs: p95 ≤ 800ms · p99 ≤ 2000ms · error ≤ 2%
🔲 6. cross-cutting 🔗
Shared vocabulary and operations that span several bounded contexts (or belong to none).
🧰 API operations (6)
▸Query: 🔎 phoneCountries🔗 #query-phonecountries
Selectable phone countries for the dialing-code picker (static reference data; the picker sends back the dialingCode '+33').
input: (none)
returns: 🧩 PhoneCountry [] · reads 🗄️ PhoneCountry
roles: EVERYONE (open — roles omitted) · V0
▸Query: 🔎 operationStatus🔗 #query-operationstatus
Poll a journaled command's status by its messageId acceptance handle (the pull counterpart of the operationStatusChanged subscription, ADR-20260720-015500). Open to every role path (roles omitted) but OWNERSHIP-SCOPED in the resolver: the row is returned only to the journaling actor (JWT subject match), the journaling session (X-SESSION-ID match — anonymous users), or ADMIN; anything else resolves null (no existence oracle). Transient — served from the command_journal, no View_*.
input: 🧩 OperationStatusQueryInput! { messageId: 🔤 MessageId }
returns: 🧩 Operation · reads —
roles: EVERYONE (open — roles omitted) · V0
▸Query: 🔎 pricingPolicy🔗 #query-pricingpolicy
The active Captain service-fee policy (admin; calibration/transparency).
input: (none)
returns: 🧩 PricingPolicy [] · reads 🗄️ PricingPolicy
roles: ADMIN · V1
▸Query: 🔎 uberEstimationPolicy🔗 #query-uberestimationpolicy
The active per-cuisine Uber Eats mark-up coefficients (admin; calibration/transparency).
input: (none)
returns: 🧩 UberEstimationPolicy [] · reads 🗄️ UberEstimationPolicy
roles: ADMIN · V1
▸Query: 🔎 uberSplitPolicy🔗 #query-ubersplitpolicy
The active Uber Eats split/fee assumptions for the estimated comparison (admin; calibration/transparency).
input: (none)
returns: 🧩 UberSplitPolicy [] · reads 🗄️ UberSplitPolicy
roles: ADMIN · V1
▸Subscription: 🔔 operationStatusChanged🔗 #subscription-operationstatuschanged
Live status of one journaled command, keyed by its messageId acceptance handle (the push counterpart of queries/operationStatus, ADR-20260720-015500). Yields the current journal state first (no subscribe/complete race), then every transition. Open to every role path (roles omitted) but ownership-scoped like the query (JWT subject / X-SESSION-ID via the WS connection_init payload / ADMIN).
input: 🧩 OperationStatusChangedSubscriptionInput! { messageId: 🔤 MessageId }
streams: 🧩 Operation
roles: EVERYONE (open — roles omitted) · V0
🧩 Output types (11)
▸Type: 🧩 Product🔗 #type-product
A catalog product grouping one or more purchasable offers.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
id🔤 ProductId✅ required
categoryRef🔤 ExternalReference⬜ nullable
name🔤 ProductName✅ required
description🔤 ProductDescription⬜ nullable
tags[🔤 Tag]✅ required
imageIds[🔤 ImageId]✅ required
taxRate📦 TaxRate✅ required
offers[🧩 Offer]✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 Offer🔗 #type-offer
A purchasable offer (SKU) of a product, with its price and derived availability.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
id🔤 OfferId✅ required
name🔤 OfferName✅ required
price📦 Money✅ required
uberPrice📦 Money⬜ nullable
uberPriceBasis🔤 ComparisonBasis⬜ nullable
availability🔤 CatalogItemAvailability✅ required
stockStatus🔤 StockStatus✅ required
optionListIds[🔤 OptionListId]✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 OptionList🔗 #type-optionlist
A modifier group (HubRise option list).
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
id🔤 OptionListId✅ required
name🔤 OptionListName✅ required
minSelectionsinteger✅ required
maxSelectionsinteger⬜ nullable
multipleSelectionboolean✅ required
options[🧩 Option]✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 Option🔗 #type-option
A selectable option within an option list.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
id🔤 OptionId✅ required
name🔤 OptionName✅ required
price📦 Money✅ required
defaultboolean✅ required
availability🔤 CatalogItemAvailability✅ required
stockStatus🔤 StockStatus⬜ nullable
▸Type: 🧩 PhoneCountry🔗 #type-phonecountry
A selectable phone country for the dialing-code picker (static reference data).
read model: 🗄️ PhoneCountry
FieldTypeReq.
country🔤 CountryCode✅ required
dialingCode🔤 DialingCode✅ required
namestring✅ required
defaultLocale🔤 Locale✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 PaymentIntent🔗 #type-paymentintent
Checkout payment state for one order (ADR-20260720-015500): the read-side home of the values placeOrder used to return, served from the PlaceOrderProcess run row by `paymentStatus` / `paymentStatusChanged`. `clientSecret` is present only while the run is in flight (NULLed when it resolves — a revocable Stripe credential, never event-sourced). NON-PROJECTED (transient): resolver-served from the saga state row, never a View_* — hence no `reads`.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
paymentIntentId🔤 PaymentIntentId✅ required
clientSecretstring⬜ nullable
status🔤 PaymentStatus✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 MutationAcceptance🔗 #type-mutationacceptance
The uniform acceptance EVERY mutation returns (acceptance-first writes, ADR-20260720-015500): the EFFECTIVE technical envelope echoed back (server-computed where the client supplied nothing) plus the journaled operation status. Business outcomes are never here — read them via queries/subscriptions (`operationStatus`, `paymentStatus`, the read models). NON-PROJECTED (transient) — built from the command_journal acceptance, no `reads`.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
messageId🔤 MessageId✅ required
correlationId🔤 CorrelationId✅ required
causeId🔤 CauseId⬜ nullable
sessionId🔤 SessionId⬜ nullable
traceId🔤 TraceId⬜ nullable
operationStatus🔤 OperationStatus✅ required
duplicateboolean✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 Operation🔗 #type-operation
Live status of a journaled command (ADR-20260720-015300), keyed by its `messageId` acceptance handle: polled by `operationStatus`, streamed by `operationStatusChanged`. `errorCode` is the stable errors.yaml code when the operation REJECTED/FAILED after acceptance (the async home of the P-10 rejection contract — sync validation failures still use GraphQL errors). Ownership- scoped in the resolver: the journaling actor (JWT), the journaling session (X-SESSION-ID), or ADMIN. NON-PROJECTED (transient) — served from the command_journal, no backing View_*.
read model: (within a parent projection)
FieldTypeReq.
messageId🔤 MessageId✅ required
correlationId🔤 CorrelationId✅ required
status🔤 OperationStatus✅ required
errorCodestring⬜ nullable
messagestring⬜ nullable
occurredAtstring date-time✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 PricingPolicy🔗 #type-pricingpolicy
The calibratable Captain service-fee policy (ADR-0016/0017); admin/transparency.
read model: 🗄️ PricingPolicy
FieldTypeReq.
currency🔤 CurrencyCode✅ required
feeRatenumber✅ required
buyerSharenumber✅ required
marginLownumber✅ required
marginHighnumber✅ required
effectiveFromstring date-time✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 UberEstimationPolicy🔗 #type-uberestimationpolicy
Calibratable per-cuisine Uber Eats mark-up coefficient (ADR-0024/0030); admin/transparency.
read model: 🗄️ UberEstimationPolicy
FieldTypeReq.
cuisineCategory🔤 CuisineCategory✅ required
priceCoefficientnumber✅ required
effectiveFromstring date-time✅ required
▸Type: 🧩 UberSplitPolicy🔗 #type-ubersplitpolicy
Calibratable Uber Eats split/fee assumptions for the estimated comparison (ADR-0024/0025/0030); admin/transparency.
read model: 🗄️ UberSplitPolicy
FieldTypeReq.
currency🔤 CurrencyCode✅ required
uberCommissionPctnumber✅ required
riderBaseCentsinteger✅ required
riderPerKmCentsinteger✅ required
avgDeliveryFeeCentsinteger✅ required
platformFeePctnumber✅ required
effectiveFromstring date-time✅ required
📦 Entities (3)
▸Entity: 📦 Courier🔗 #entity-courier
The person delivering an order. For an INDEPENDENT rider, `riderId` is set (a Captain RIDER); for a PARTNER courier (e.g. Avelo37) only `displayName`/`phone` are known, reported by the partner.
FieldTypeReq.Description
displayNamestring✅ required
phone🔤 PhoneNumber⬜ nullable
riderId🔤 RiderId⬜ nullableSet when the courier is an independent Captain rider; null for a partner courier.
▸Entity: 📦 TaxRate🔗 #entity-taxrate
Per-service-mode VAT, mirroring HubRise product tax_rate.
FieldTypeReq.Description
delivery🔤 TaxRatePercent✅ required
collection🔤 TaxRatePercent⬜ nullable
eatIn🔤 TaxRatePercent⬜ nullable
▸Entity: 📦 Address🔗 #entity-address
FieldTypeReq.Description
line1🔤 AddressLine✅ required
line2🔤 AddressLine⬜ nullable
postalCode🔤 PostalCode✅ required
city🔤 CityName✅ required
country🔤 CountryCode✅ required
🔤 Scalars (40)
ScalarTypeDescription
🔤 RestaurantIdstring uuidA single restaurant location (HubRise: location), belonging to a RestaurantAccount.
🔤 ProductIdstring uuidA product groups one or more offers (HubRise: product).
🔤 SessionIdstring uuidA session is a temporary auth token (ADR-0041) for a visitor or customer, used to bind carts and track the user across devices.
🔤 OfferIdstring uuidA purchasable offer with its own price (HubRise: SKU).
🔤 OrderIdstring uuid
🔤 CustomerIdstring uuid
🔤 CorrelationIdstring uuidCorrelates a command with the events/state it produces. Returned by every mutation payload so the client can track the outcome via the read side (matches domain_events.correlation_id).
🔤 CauseIdstring uuidCausation id: the id of the message/event that directly caused this one (matches domain_events.cause_id). Threads a cause→effect chain through the log; null for a root command.
🔤 MessageIdstring uuidUnique id of one command submission — the idempotency key of the write path (command_journal pk, ADR-20260720-015300). Client-suppliable via MetadataInput; server-generated (UUIDv7) when absent. A replayed messageId with an identical payload acknowledges against the original; the same id with a different payload is rejected (Conflict). Events emitted by the command carry it as domain_events.cause_id.
🔤 TraceIdstring ^[0-9a-f]{32}$W3C trace-id (from the inbound `traceparent` header, or server-started). Response-only technical tracing identifier — never client-suppliable, never a substitute for correlationId (P-02).
🔤 ExternalReferencestringExternal reference code (HubRise `ref`), unique within its scope. Used for idempotent import/sync and as a stable reference inside orders. Example: 'MARGHERITA', 'CAT-PIZZAS'.
🔤 Slugstring ^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$URL-safe identifier for restaurants. Lowercase, dash-separated. Example: 'chez-marco', 'tokyo-sushi'.
🔤 RestaurantDisplayNamestringPublic label shown to customers. Can be short and brand-oriented. Example: 'Chez Marco', 'Tokyo Sushi'.
🔤 ProductNamestring
🔤 OfferNamestringOffer label, unique within a product (HubRise SKU name). Example: 'Small', 'Large', 'Default'.
🔤 OptionNamestring
🔤 TagstringFree-form label (HubRise: tags). Example: 'vegan', 'spicy', 'eco'.
🔤 EmailAddressstring email
🔤 PhoneNumberstringCanonical E.164 phone (e.g. '+33612345678'). Composed server-side from DialingCode + NationalPhoneNumber and stored on events/views. Validation enforced at application level.
🔤 AddressLinestring
🔤 PostalCodestring
🔤 CountryCodestring ^[A-Z]{2}$ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Example: 'FR'.
🔤 TimeZonestringIANA timezone. Example: 'Europe/Paris'.
🔤 CurrencyCodestring ^[A-Z]{3}$ISO 4217 currency code. Example: 'EUR'.
🔤 TaxRatePercentnumberPercentage tax rate. Example: 10.0 for 10%.
🔤 QuantitynumberStock quantity in units. Decimals allowed to match HubRise inventory. 0 means out of stock.
🔤 CustomerDisplayNamestring
🔤 ServiceTypeenum (DELIVERY | COLLECTION)Aligned with HubRise service types. COLLECTION == customer pickup. (EAT_IN is not offered by Captain.Food but is kept in TaxRate for catalog fidelity.)
🔤 DeliveryStatusenum (PENDING | ASSIGNED | PICKED_UP | OUT_FOR_DELIVERY | DELIVERED | FAILED | CANCELLED)Status of one delivery, reported by the partner (inbound) or driven by an independent rider's commands.
🔤 OperationStatusenum (PENDING | SUCCEEDED | REJECTED | FAILED)Live status of a command/operation streamed by the operationStatusChanged subscription: PENDING (accepted, in flight), SUCCEEDED, REJECTED (business invariant), FAILED (technical error)."
🔤 CommandJournalStatusenum (RECEIVED | SUCCEEDED | REJECTED | FAILED)Lifecycle of a journaled command: RECEIVED (durably accepted, handler spawned), then SUCCEEDED, REJECTED (business invariant) or FAILED (technical). Maps onto the API OperationStatus (RECEIVED → PENDING). A duplicate submission is an acceptance-response attribute, not a status — the journal row keeps the original's real state.
🔤 CommandChannelenum (GRAPHQL | WORKER | INTERNAL)Surface a journaled command arrived through: the GraphQL BFF dispatch, an on-app drain/enrichment worker (e.g. the HubRise enricher), or an internal trigger endpoint.
🔤 InboundEventStatusenum (RECEIVED | DELIVERED | FAILED)Lifecycle of an adapted inbound business event (inbound_events row): RECEIVED (staged by the adapter ACL), DELIVERED (appended through the normal write path — includes the aggregate's already-recorded no-op), FAILED (delivery error, left for retry/inspection).
🔤 PaymentProcessStatusenum (AWAITING_PAYMENT_RESULT | ORDER_PLACED | FAILED)State of one PlaceOrderProcess checkout run (payment_process_manager row, keyed by cart).
🔤 RefundProcessStatusenum (PENDING_APPROVAL | APPROVED_AWAITING_SETTLEMENT | DENIED | REFUNDED)State of one RefundProcess run (refund_process_manager row, keyed by order). Refunds are approved by the restaurant (own orders) or an admin.
🔤 DeliveryDispatchProcessStatusenum (OFFERED | ACCEPTED | FAILED | COMPLETED | SELF_DISPATCHED)State of one DeliveryDispatchProcess run (delivery_dispatch_process_manager row, keyed by order). FAILED now marks the ranked channel walk exhausted (fail closed, rules.yaml#/DispatchExhaustionFailsClosed — #60 supersedes the ADR-20260720-004556 numeric cap-3 framing). SELF_DISPATCHED is appended LAST to preserve the declaration-order ordinals of the pre-#60 values (ADR-0037)."
🔤 RestaurantDispatchModeenum (CAPTAIN | RESTAURANT)Who is responsible for fulfilling a restaurant's deliveries (restaurant-scoped config, resolved at runtime; #60). Default CAPTAIN keeps today's behaviour.
🔤 DeliveryChannelKindenum (POOL | PARTNER)Kind of a DeliveryChannelCatalog entry — POOL (independent riders) vs PARTNER (adapter-backed). Every PARTNER channel must have a wired services.yaml delivery implementation (#60).
🔤 Modeenum (LIVE | TEST)Whether an aggregate is production (LIVE) or a non-production TEST fixture coexisting in prod (ADR-0038, Stripe-`livemode`-style). Set at creation, immutable; absent = LIVE. TEST data is isolated from payouts, analytics and real notifications; a TEST order may target a LIVE restaurant to validate the real receipt path.
🔤 UserTypeenum (PUBLIC | CUSTOMER | RESTAURANT_ACCOUNT | RESTAURANT | RIDER | ADMIN | EXTERNAL)
⛔ Errors (10)
ErrorDescriptionMessage (en)Message (fr)Thrown by
⛔ UnauthorizedNo valid authentication for this path/role.🇬🇧 You must be signed in to do this.🇫🇷 Vous devez être connecté pour effectuer cette action.—
⛔ ForbiddenAuthenticated, but not allowed to act on this resource (e.g. not the owner).🇬🇧 You are not allowed to perform this action.🇫🇷 Vous n'êtes pas autorisé à effectuer cette action.—
⛔ ValidationErrorInput failed schema validation (type, format, required, bounds).🇬🇧 The field '{field}' is invalid.🇫🇷 Le champ '{field}' est invalide.—
⛔ ConflictConcurrent modification (optimistic-concurrency version clash); retry.🇬🇧 This item was modified meanwhile. Please retry.🇫🇷 Cet élément a été modifié entre-temps. Veuillez réessayer.—
⛔ RateLimitedToo many requests on this path.🇬🇧 Too many requests. Please slow down.🇫🇷 Trop de requêtes. Veuillez patienter.—
⛔ InternalUnexpected server error.🇬🇧 Something went wrong on our side.🇫🇷 Une erreur est survenue de notre côté.—
⛔ RestaurantNotFoundNo restaurant with this id.🇬🇧 Restaurant not found.🇫🇷 Restaurant introuvable.📩 ActivateRestaurant, 📩 UpdateRestaurant, 📩 DeactivateRestaurant, 📩 ChangeOrderAcceptanceMode, 📩 RemoveRestaurant, 📩 UpdateRestaurantGoogleBusinessProfile, 📩 MarkRestaurantClosed, 📩 ClaimRestaurantListing, 📩 OptOutRestaurantListing, 📩 ChangeRestaurantListingStatus, 📩 ConfigureGoogleBusinessProfileOrderLink, 📩 VerifyGoogleBusinessProfileOrderLink, 📩 CreateCatalog, 📩 MarkRestaurantAsFavorite
⛔ NoEditableFieldProvidedUpdate command carried no editable field.🇬🇧 Provide at least one field to update.🇫🇷 Indiquez au moins un champ à modifier.📩 UpdateRestaurantAccount, 📩 UpdateRestaurant, 📩 UpdateCustomerInfo, 📩 UpdateRiderInfo
⛔ OfferNotFoundNo offer with this id in the catalog.🇬🇧 Product offer not found.🇫🇷 Offere de produit introuvable.📩 UpdateOfferStock, 📩 AddCartLine
⛔ PaymentEventOrphanedA Stripe payment outcome (capture or failure) references a PaymentIntent that matches no known checkout run. The inbound fact stays recorded on the Payment, but the process manager aborts and surfaces this error for ops attention (money may have been taken with no order to materialize) — an anomaly is never silently skipped. 🇬🇧 Payment event received for an unknown checkout.🇫🇷 Événement de paiement reçu pour un checkout inconnu.—
📡 Observability (5)
▸Contract: 📡 command-acceptance🔗 #obs-command-acceptance
criticality: high
workflow: surface graphql (dispatch pipeline)
emits: — · inbound: —
run identity
IdSourceReq.Business key
correlation_idcommand.correlation_id✅—
trace_idotel.trace_id✅—
message_idcommand.message_id✅—
command_typecommand.type✅—
channeljournal.channel✅—
spans (* = required attribute)
SpanKindReq.MultiplicityAttributes
command.receiveSERVER✅—business.command_type*, business.actor*, business.channel*
command.journalINTERNAL✅—business.message_id*, business.journal_status*
command.dispatchINTERNAL✅—business.message_id*, business.dispatch_outcome*
metrics: commands_accepted_total (counter), command_duplicates_total (counter), command_sync_conflicts_total (counter), command_completion_ms (histogram) · business: —
success ⇐ spans: command.receive, command.journal, command.dispatch
SLOs: p95 ≤ 150ms · p99 ≤ 400ms · error ≤ 0.5%
▸Contract: 📡 stripe-webhook-ingestion🔗 #obs-stripe-webhook-ingestion
criticality: high
workflow:
emits: — · inbound: ⚡ PaymentCaptured, ⚡ PaymentFailed, ⚡ PaymentRefunded
run identity
IdSourceReq.Business key
correlation_idinbound.correlation_id✅—
trace_idotel.trace_id✅—
inbound_event_idinbound.inbound_event_id✅—
external_event_idstripe.event_id✅—
spans (* = required attribute)
SpanKindReq.MultiplicityAttributes
webhook.verifySERVER✅—business.source*, business.signature_status*
external.persistINTERNAL✅—business.external_event_id*, business.dedupe*
acl.translateINTERNAL✅—business.event_type*
inbound.persistINTERNAL✅—business.inbound_event_id*
inbound.drain.deliverCONSUMER✅—business.inbound_event_id*, business.delivery_outcome*
event.store.appendINTERNAL✅—business.event_type*, business.stream_id*
metrics: stripe_webhook_ingest_duration_ms (histogram), inbound_drain_lag_ms (histogram) · business: inbound_events_staged_total (counter), inbound_events_delivered_total (counter), webhook_duplicates_total (counter)
success ⇐ spans: webhook.verify, external.persist, acl.translate, inbound.persist, inbound.drain.deliver, event.store.append
SLOs: p95 ≤ 1000ms · p99 ≤ 3000ms · error ≤ 1%

📱 Restaurant front-office screens (SDUI) 🔗

Server-Driven UI screens (restaurant_frontoffice.yaml, ADR-0033). Per screen, the reads (resolvers→queries) and writes (actions→mutations) are $ref-bound to the GraphQL API and validated — the mockups are the proof the API answers the UI. ⚠️ marks gaps the API does not serve yet; 🚫 screens are intentionally not SDUI-rendered.

▸Screen: 📱 home / 📱 SDUI🔗
📱 Home · /
«topbar»
hero_search_bar Search restaurants or dishes…
horizontal_scroll
section What are you craving?
section Featured near you
section All restaurants
cta_banner Are you a restaurant?
«cart_fab»
«bottom_nav»
UI needGraphQL operation
readpromotions.active⚠️ No promotions/deals query — HubRise deals not modelled in V0 (home promo banners).
readcategories.all🔎 categories
readrestaurants.featured🔎 restaurants
readrestaurants.all🔎 restaurants

Gaps

  • ⚠️ `promotions.active` (promo banners) has no backing query — deals deferred.
▸Screen: 📱 search /search 📱 SDUI🔗
📱 Search · /search
«topbar»
search_bar_active Restaurants, dishes, cuisines…
conditional_section
«bottom_nav»
UI needGraphQL operation
readrestaurants.search🔎 restaurants
readcategories.all🔎 categories
readdishes.search⚠️ No dish/product search query — only restaurant search (queries/restaurants) exists.

Gaps

  • ⚠️ `dishes.search` (the "Dishes" results) has no backing query — only restaurant search exists.
▸Screen: 📱 restaurant /r/:slug 📱 SDUI🔗
📱 {{ restaurant.name }} · /r/:slug
back_button_header
hero_image
section
sticky_category_nav
catalog_sections
spacer
«cart_fab»
«bottom_nav»
UI needGraphQL operation
readrestaurant.bySlug🔎 restaurant
readcatalog.byRestaurant🔎 catalog
writetoggle_favorite✏️ markRestaurantAsFavorite

Gaps

  • ⚠️ Restaurant presentation fields the UI reads (coverUrl, logoUrl, deliveryTime, deliveryFee, minimumOrder, badges, isOpen) are not on the domain Restaurant/api type yet.
▸Screen: 📱 cart /cart 📱 SDUI🔗
📱 Your cart · /cart
back_button_header Your cart
cart_lines
promo_code_input Promo code
delivery_mode_toggle
order_summary_block
minimum_order_warning
sticky_bottom_bar
UI needGraphQL operation
readcart.current🔎 cart
writechange_cart_line_quantity✏️ changeCartLineQuantity
writeapply_promo_code⚠️ No applyPromoCode mutation — promo codes / cart discount not modelled.
writeset_delivery_mode⚠️ Cart-level delivery-mode toggle not modelled — serviceType is chosen at placeOrder.

Gaps

  • ⚠️ `apply_promo_code` action + cart.discount/promoCode have no backing mutation — promo codes not modelled.
  • ⚠️ `set_delivery_mode` action has no backing mutation — serviceType is chosen at placeOrder, not stored on the cart.
▸Screen: 📱 checkout /checkout 🚫 not SDUI🔒 auth🔗
Stripe Elements + payment security — a static React page, not runtime JSON.
📱 Checkout · /checkout
back_button_header Checkout
checkout_section Delivery details
checkout_section Contact
checkout_section Delivery instructions
checkout_section Order summary
checkout_section Payment
sticky_bottom_bar
UI needGraphQL operation
readcart.current🔎 cart
readme.profile🔎 me
writeplace_order✏️ placeOrder
writeset_delivery_address_saved✏️ setCustomerAddress
▸Screen: 📱 order_tracking /orders/:orderId/confirmation 🚫 not SDUI🔒 auth🔗
Realtime GraphQL subscription + order state machine — a static React page.
📱 Order tracking · /orders/:orderId/confirmation
order_status_hero
eta_bar Estimated arrival
order_timeline
restaurant_contact_row
order_items_summary
order_id_row Order ID
section
UI needGraphQL operation
readorder.byId🔎 order
writereorder⚠️ No reorder op — client re-adds past order lines via add_to_cart.
writerate_order✏️ rateOrder

Gaps

  • ⚠️ `reorder` action has no backing op — the client re-adds past lines via add_to_cart.
▸Screen: 📱 order_history /orders 📱 SDUI🔒 auth🔗

«topbar»
page_header Your orders

order_list

readorders.mine🔎 orders

«topbar»
account_header
menu_section My account
menu_section Captain.Food
menu_section Support
sign_out_button Sign out
«bottom_nav»
UI needGraphQL operation
readme.profile🔎 me
readfavorites.mine🔎 favoriteRestaurants
readrewards.balance⚠️ Captain Coins / loyalty balance not modelled (deferred domain).
writeopen_intercom⚠️ Support chat (Intercom) is a third-party widget, not a domain op.
writeset_delivery_address_saved✏️ setCustomerAddress

Gaps

  • ⚠️ `rewards.balance` (Captain Coins), referral and passkeys/notifications management are not modelled (deferred domains).

cta_section
🌐 Translations 🔗

The i18n catalog (translations.yaml) — every screen string, referenced by $ref, generated to one translations.generated.json. {param} tokens are validated against declared params.

KeyParamsenfr
🌐 common.nav.home—🇬🇧 Home🇫🇷 Accueil
🌐 common.nav.search—🇬🇧 Search🇫🇷 Recherche
🌐 common.nav.orders—🇬🇧 Orders🇫🇷 Commandes
🌐 common.nav.account—🇬🇧 Account🇫🇷 Compte
🌐 common.cart.view—🇬🇧 View cart🇫🇷 Voir le panier
🌐 common.action.change—🇬🇧 Change🇫🇷 Modifier
🌐 common.action.apply—🇬🇧 Apply🇫🇷 Appliquer
🌐 common.action.submit—🇬🇧 Submit🇫🇷 Envoyer
🌐 common.action.see_all—🇬🇧 See all🇫🇷 Tout voir
🌐 common.filter.sort—🇬🇧 Sort🇫🇷 Trier
🌐 common.delivery.free—🇬🇧 Free delivery🇫🇷 Livraison gratuite
🌐 common.mode.delivery—🇬🇧 Delivery🇫🇷 Livraison
🌐 common.mode.pickup—🇬🇧 Pickup🇫🇷 À emporter
🌐 location.title—🇬🇧 Delivery address🇫🇷 Adresse de livraison
🌐 location.search_placeholder—🇬🇧 Search for an address…🇫🇷 Rechercher une adresse…
🌐 location.recent—🇬🇧 Recent🇫🇷 Récentes
🌐 location.saved—🇬🇧 Saved🇫🇷 Enregistrées
🌐 location.use_current—🇬🇧 Use current location🇫🇷 Utiliser ma position
🌐 auth.title—🇬🇧 Sign in or create account🇫🇷 Se connecter ou créer un compte
🌐 auth.body—🇬🇧 Enter your phone number to continue. No password needed.🇫🇷 Entrez votre numéro de téléphone pour continuer. Aucun mot de passe requis.
🌐 auth.phone_label—🇬🇧 Phone number🇫🇷 Numéro de téléphone
🌐 auth.continue—🇬🇧 Continue🇫🇷 Continuer
🌐 auth.or—🇬🇧 or🇫🇷 ou
🌐 auth.passkey—🇬🇧 Continue with passkey🇫🇷 Continuer avec une clé d'accès
🌐 auth.otp_title—🇬🇧 Verify your number🇫🇷 Vérifiez votre numéro
🌐 auth.otp_bodyphone🇬🇧 We sent a code to {phone}. Enter it below.🇫🇷 Nous avons envoyé un code au {phone}. Saisissez-le ci-dessous.
🌐 auth.resend—🇬🇧 Resend code🇫🇷 Renvoyer le code
🌐 auth.resend_inseconds🇬🇧 Resend in {seconds}s🇫🇷 Renvoyer dans {seconds}s
🌐 item.choose_one—🇬🇧 Choose 1🇫🇷 Choisissez 1
🌐 item.instructions_placeholder—🇬🇧 Special instructions (optional)🇫🇷 Instructions spéciales (facultatif)
🌐 item.add_to_carttotal🇬🇧 Add to cart — {total}🇫🇷 Ajouter au panier — {total}
🌐 rating.title—🇬🇧 Rate your order🇫🇷 Évaluez votre commande
🌐 rating.food_question—🇬🇧 How was the food?🇫🇷 Comment était le repas ?
🌐 rating.tags_label—🇬🇧 What went well? (optional)🇫🇷 Qu'est-ce qui vous a plu ? (facultatif)
🌐 rating.tag.taste—🇬🇧 Great taste🇫🇷 Très bon goût
🌐 rating.tag.portions—🇬🇧 Good portions🇫🇷 Bonnes portions
🌐 rating.tag.fast—🇬🇧 Fast delivery🇫🇷 Livraison rapide
🌐 rating.tag.accurate—🇬🇧 Accurate order🇫🇷 Commande conforme
🌐 rating.tag.packaging—🇬🇧 Great packaging🇫🇷 Bon emballage
🌐 rating.comment_placeholder—🇬🇧 Add a comment (optional)🇫🇷 Ajouter un commentaire (facultatif)
🌐 rating.thanks—🇬🇧 Thanks for your feedback!🇫🇷 Merci pour votre retour !
🌐 home.search_placeholder—🇬🇧 Search restaurants or dishes…🇫🇷 Rechercher un restaurant ou un plat…
🌐 home.craving—🇬🇧 What are you craving?🇫🇷 Une envie particulière ?
🌐 home.featured—🇬🇧 Featured near you🇫🇷 En vedette près de chez vous

🌐 search.placeholder—🇬🇧 Restaurants, dishes, cuisines…🇫🇷 Restaurants, plats, cuisines…
🌐 search.recent—🇬🇧 Recent searches🇫🇷 Recherches récentes
🌐 search.popular—🇬🇧 Popular categories🇫🇷 Catégories populaires
🌐 search.restaurants—🇬🇧 Restaurants🇫🇷 Restaurants
🌐 search.dishes—🇬🇧 Dishes🇫🇷 Plats

🌐 search.empty.body—🇬🇧 Try a different dish or restaurant name.🇫🇷 Essayez un autre plat ou nom de restaurant.

🌐 restaurant.closed—🇬🇧 Closed🇫🇷 Fermé

🌐 cart.empty.browse—🇬🇧 Browse restaurants🇫🇷 Parcourir les restaurants
🌐 cart.promo_placeholder—🇬🇧 Promo code🇫🇷 Code promo
🌐 cart.promo_appliedcode, amount🇬🇧 Code {code} applied — {amount} off🇫🇷 Code {code} appliqué — {amount} de réduction

🌐 order.tracking_title—🇬🇧 Order tracking🇫🇷 Suivi de commande
🌐 order.status.placed.title—🇬🇧 Order placed!🇫🇷 Commande passée !
🌐 order.status.placed.bodyrestaurant🇬🇧 Your order is being sent to {restaurant}.🇫🇷 Votre commande est envoyée à {restaurant}.
🌐 order.status.accepted.title—🇬🇧 Order accepted🇫🇷 Commande acceptée
🌐 order.status.accepted.bodyrestaurant🇬🇧 {restaurant} is preparing your food.🇫🇷 {restaurant} prépare votre commande.
🌐 order.status.rejected.title—🇬🇧 Order rejected🇫🇷 Commande refusée

🌐 order.status.ready.title—🇬🇧 Ready for pickup!🇫🇷 Prêt à récupérer !
🌐 order.status.ready.body—🇬🇧 Your order is ready.🇫🇷 Votre commande est prête.
🌐 order.status.out_for_delivery.title—🇬🇧 On the way!🇫🇷 En route !
🌐 order.status.out_for_delivery.body—🇬🇧 Your delivery is en route.🇫🇷 Votre livraison est en route.
🌐 order.status.delivered.title—🇬🇧 Delivered!🇫🇷 Livré !
🌐 order.status.delivered.body—🇬🇧 Enjoy your meal. 🎉🇫🇷 Bon appétit. 🎉
🌐 order.status.cancelled.title—🇬🇧 Order cancelled🇫🇷 Commande annulée

🌐 partner.header—🇬🇧 Partner with us🇫🇷 Devenez partenaire
🌐 partner.hero_title—🇬🇧 Grow your restaurant with Captain.Food🇫🇷 Développez votre restaurant avec Captain.Food
🌐 partner.hero_body—🇬🇧 Join Tours' leading food platform. No commission model — flat subscription.🇫🇷 Rejoignez la première plateforme food de Tours. Sans commission — abonnement forfaitaire.
🌐 partner.prop.orders.title—🇬🇧 Increase orders🇫🇷 Augmentez vos commandes
🌐 partner.prop.orders.body—🇬🇧 Reach thousands of new customers in your city.🇫🇷 Touchez des milliers de nouveaux clients dans votre ville.
🌐 partner.prop.margins.title—🇬🇧 Keep your margins🇫🇷 Préservez vos marges
🌐 partner.prop.margins.body—🇬🇧 Flat monthly fee. No per-order commission.🇫🇷 Forfait mensuel fixe. Aucune commission par commande.
🌐 partner.prop.control.title—🇬🇧 Full control🇫🇷 Contrôle total
🌐 partner.prop.control.body—🇬🇧 Manage your menu, hours, and catalog in real-time.🇫🇷 Gérez votre menu, vos horaires et votre catalogue en temps réel.
🌐 partner.apply—🇬🇧 Apply to join🇫🇷 Postuler
🏛️ Architecture (C4) 🔗
system: Captain.Food — Local-first food ordering & delivery for independent restaurants and food trucks (V0: Tours).

🔲 L2 — Bounded contexts

ContextDescriptionAggregates / process managers
🔲 restaurantRestaurant provider domain: accounts, locations, lifecycle, order-acceptance mode (incl. catalog & order-fulfilment operations performed by restaurant staff).🎭 RestaurantAccount, 🎭 Restaurant, 🎭 Prospect
🔲 catalogCatalog tree, products, offers (SKUs), option lists, per-offer stock; HubRise import.🎭 Catalog
🔲 orderCart selection → checkout → order lifecycle, incl. the checkout & refund sagas (the V0 risk point: external Stripe).🎭 Cart, 🎭 Order, 🎭 Payment · 📦 PlaceOrderProcess, 📦 RefundProcess
🔲 customerCustomer-facing consumer domain: discovery/browse, identity (phone-keyed), favorites, profile, address book, cart & ordering use-cases; cart binding.🎭 Customer · 📦 CartBindingProcess
🔲 deliveryDelivery fulfilment: dispatch of ready DELIVERY orders to a partner (Avelo37) and/or independent riders, courier assignment, status tracking to hand-over (ADR-0031).🎭 DeliveryJob, 🎭 Rider, 🎭 DeliveryPartnerRegistration · 📦 DeliveryDispatchProcess

🧱 L2 — Containers

ContainerTechnologyDescription
🧱 web-clientLeptos (Rust → WASM), Crux core, SSR+hydrationCustomer mobile-first web client (SDUI renderer, ADR-0033/0034); multi-tenant via {restaurantSlug}.captain.food.
🧱 web-restaurantLeptos (Rust → WASM), Crux coreRestaurant web app/dashboard: onboarding (incl. Google Business Profile 'Order online' setup — ADR-019), catalog, order queue, payouts (/restaurant-account/graphql, /restaurant/graphql).
🧱 web-adminLeptos (Rust → WASM), Crux corePlatform back-office for Captain.Food staff (/admin/graphql): restaurant approvals, pre-registration pipeline, ops.
🧱 desktop-restaurantTauri 2.0 shell + Leptos + Crux core (Rust)Restaurant-manager desktop app (ADR-0034): the web-restaurant UI in a native shell, same Rust core in-process.
🧱 mobile-customerSwiftUI (iOS) / Jetpack Compose (Android) thin shells → Crux core via UniFFI (Rust)Customer mobile app (post-V0); thin native UI over the shared Rust core (ADR-0034); same GraphQL API (/customer/graphql, /public/graphql).
🧱 mobile-restaurantSwiftUI / Jetpack Compose thin shells → Crux core via UniFFI (Rust)Restaurant-staff mobile app (post-V0): order queue, accept/ready (/restaurant/graphql).
🧱 mobile-riderSwiftUI / Jetpack Compose thin shells → Crux core via UniFFI (Rust)Delivery-rider mobile app (post-V0): assigned deliveries + status updates (/rider/graphql).
🧱 apiRust — Axum + Tokio + SQLx + async-graphql (BFF over the Crux core)CQRS-light write+read API (ADR-0034). Hosts command handlers, projections, GraphQL gateway. Role = path (/{role}/graphql).
realizes: 🎭 RestaurantAccount, 🎭 Restaurant, 🎭 Prospect, 🎭 Catalog, 🎭 Cart, 🎭 Order, 🎭 Customer, 📦 PlaceOrderProcess, 📦 RefundProcess, 📦 CartBindingProcess, 🎭 DeliveryJob, 📦 DeliveryDispatchProcess
🧱 event-storeManaged PostgreSQL (e.g. Supabase)Append-only domain_events table (the write model / source of truth at runtime).
🧱 read-modelsManaged PostgreSQLDenormalized View_* projection tables fed from the event log; queries read here, never domain_events.
🧱 sync-workerScheduled worker (GitHub Actions cron + Rust binary, shared Crux core)Restaurant listing sync (ADR-0020): polls INSEE Sirene + Google Maps and, via the ACL, calls the api's RegisterRestaurant / UpdateRestaurantGoogleBusinessProfile / MarkRestaurantClosed as the owner. Prospection scoring/outreach is a later step.
🧱 bamProjection workerBusiness Activity Monitoring projector — consumes the same event stream to answer business questions.
🧱 otel-collectorOpenTelemetry CollectorReceives traces/metrics/logs from the api and bam containers; exports to the backend(s).

🔌 L2 — External systems

SystemDescription
🔌 stripePayments (PaymentIntent capture, refunds); later Stripe Connect.
🔌 hubriseExisting restaurant catalog/orders systems; import via the Anti-Corruption Layer.
🔌 delivery-partnerDelivery partner (e.g. Avelo37): dispatch delivery jobs out, receive courier/status facts inbound via the avelo37-acl (ADR-0031).
🔌 supabase-authPasswordless OTP identity for customers (not a domain concern).
🔌 sireneINSEE Sirene / Recherche d'Entreprises (Etalab open data): SIRET, name, address, NAF, active/closed. Seeds listings via the ACL.
🔌 google-mapsGoogle Maps Places / Business Profile: rating, reviews, hours, phone, website, place id, and the 'Order online' link (ADR-0021). Enrichment + ownership proof.
🔌 hubspotCRM for B2B prospection leads (ADR-0020); post-V0.
🔌 resendTransactional email for prospection outreach sequences (ADR-0020); post-V0.
🔌 slackOps/prospection alerts (active-partner closures, sequence events); post-V0.

➡️ L2 — Relationships

EdgeDescription
web-client → apiGraphQL over HTTPS (/customer/graphql, /public/graphql)
web-restaurant → apiGraphQL (/restaurant-account/graphql, /restaurant/graphql)
web-admin → apiGraphQL (/admin/graphql)
desktop-restaurant → apiGraphQL (/restaurant-account/graphql, /restaurant/graphql) — Tauri shell
mobile-customer → apiGraphQL (/customer/graphql, /public/graphql) — post-V0
mobile-restaurant → apiGraphQL (/restaurant/graphql) — post-V0
mobile-rider → apiGraphQL (/rider/graphql) — post-V0
api → event-storeAppend domain events (command side)
api → read-modelsRead projections (query side) + projection updates
api → stripeCreate PaymentIntents, request refunds; receive webhooks (inbound facts)
api → hubriseImport catalog / sync inventory via ACL (inbound facts)
api → supabase-authOTP verify / session (out of domain)
api → delivery-partnerDispatch delivery jobs; receive courier acceptance/status webhooks (inbound facts) — ADR-0031
sync-worker → sirenePoll establishments (SIRET/NAF/address/closures)
sync-worker → google-mapsFetch Business Profile data (rating/reviews/hours/website)
sync-worker → apiRegister/enrich/close listings + record prospect contacts via the ACL (/external/graphql)
api → google-mapsVerify restaurant ownership (GBP) for claim/opt-out
sync-worker → hubspotCreate/update prospection leads (ADR-0020)
sync-worker → resendSend prospection outreach emails (ADR-0020)
sync-worker → slackProspection / ops alerts (ADR-0020)
bam → event-storeConsume the event stream for business metrics
api → otel-collectorExport traces/metrics/logs (OTLP)
bam → otel-collectorExport traces/metrics/logs (OTLP)

⚙️ L3 — Components of the api container

ComponentInstrumentedDescriptionBinds
⚙️ graphql-gateway📡 yesPer-role GraphQL endpoint (/{role}/graphql); applies the @auth/@public ACL; entry span (SERVER).—
⚙️ observability-middleware📡 yesAttaches business.* attributes + correlation/cause ids to spans; structured JSON logging; the only place domain context meets OTel.—
⚙️ command-bus📡 yesDispatches commands to handlers; span 'command.receive'/'command.validate'/'command.handle'.—
⚙️ command-handlers— noOne handler per aggregate; validates invariants then appends events. Pure domain — NOT instrumented.handles 🎭 RestaurantAccount, 🎭 Restaurant, 🎭 Prospect, 🎭 Catalog, 🎭 Cart, 🎭 Order, 🎭 Customer, 🎭 DeliveryJob
⚙️ process-managers📡 yesSagas coordinating aggregates + externals (checkout, refund, cart binding, delivery dispatch).handles 📦 PlaceOrderProcess, 📦 RefundProcess, 📦 CartBindingProcess, 📦 DeliveryDispatchProcess
⚙️ event-store-adapter📡 yesAppends to domain_events; span 'event.store.append' with business.event_type/stream_id.—
⚙️ event-publisher📡 yesPublishes appended events to the bus; span 'event.publish' (PRODUCER).—
⚙️ message-consumers📡 yesConsume domain + inbound integration events; span 'event.consume.*' (CONSUMER).—
⚙️ projection-updaters📡 yesUpdate the View_* read models from events; span 'event.consume.projection'.updates 🗄️ View_RestaurantAccount, 🗄️ Restaurant, 🗄️ Customer, 🗄️ Catalog, 🗄️ Cart, 🗄️ OrderTracking, 🗄️ ProspectionPipeline, 🗄️ View_DeliveryJob
⚙️ bam-projector📡 yesBusiness Activity Monitoring projection (runs in the bam container); business_metrics only.—
⚙️ hubrise-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer translating HubRise payloads (SKU/option_list/'9.80 EUR') into the domain.—
⚙️ stripe-adapter📡 yesStripe Connect (Separate Charges & Transfers, transfer_group=ORDER_{id}; Captain = merchant of record): creates the PaymentIntent for the buyer total, then after capture transfers restaurantPayout/riderPayout to the connected accounts (3-way split, ADR-0017), keeping captainNet on the platform; refunds reverse the transfers. Records inbound webhook facts (PaymentCaptured/Failed/Refunded).—
⚙️ supabase-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer wrapping Supabase Auth (ADR-0015): sends/verifies phone OTP (Twilio; mock in dev) and email magic links SYNCHRONOUSLY, validates tokens server-side, and translates the Supabase user (id/phone/email) into the domain (authRef). Keeps the Supabase SDK out of the aggregates.—
⚙️ sirene-google-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer translating INSEE Sirene + Google Maps data into Restaurant commands (RegisterRestaurant / UpdateRestaurantGoogleBusinessProfile / MarkRestaurantClosed) as the owner, and validating Google Business Profile ownership proofs for claim/opt-out (ADR-0019/0021). Keeps Sirene/Google SDKs out of the aggregate.—
⚙️ prospection-acl📡 yesB2B prospection worker (ADR-0020): reads the COMPUTED score from ProspectionPipeline, applies the J+0/J+7/J+21 schedule + anti-spam, fires HubSpot/Resend/Slack, then issues RecordProspectContact / MarkProspectCold to record the facts. The score is never an input it stores back.—
⚙️ avelo37-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer for the delivery partner (Avelo37; ADR-0031): on DeliveryRequested, dispatches the job to the partner API; translates the partner's webhooks into the inbound facts DeliveryAcceptedByPartner / DeliveryRejectedByPartner / DeliveryStatusUpdated (idempotent on partnerRef). Keeps the partner SDK out of the domain; mirrors stripe-adapter.—
⚙️ coopcycle-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer for the CoopCycle delivery federation (issue #58, ADR-20260721-122910): the third PARTNER seam, mirroring avelo37-acl. FEDERATION — CoopCycle is many self-hosted co-op instances, so the outbound offer_job resolves a job to an instance (per-instance base URL + OAuth2 client-credentials, env-gated) and each instance's verified webhook (per-instance secret) is translated into the same inbound facts DeliveryAcceptedByPartner / DeliveryRejectedByPartner / DeliveryStatusUpdated (idempotent on partnerRef). Keeps the partner SDK out of the domain.—
⚙️ uber_direct-acl📡 yesAnti-Corruption Layer for the Uber Direct delivery partner (issue #57, ADR-20260721-172500): a PARTNER seam via the Uber DIRECT delivery API (not the Uber Eats marketplace), mirroring avelo37-acl. ONE central API (no federation): the outbound offer_job fetches an OAuth2 client-credentials token (env-gated by UBER_DIRECT_*) and POSTs a Create Delivery; Uber's verified webhook (X-Uber-Signature raw-body HMAC) is translated into the same inbound facts DeliveryAcceptedByPartner / DeliveryRejectedByPartner / DeliveryStatusUpdated (idempotent on partnerRef). Keeps the partner SDK out of the domain.—
🗺️ System map (interactive) 🔗

Drill in: System → container → bounded context → aggregate flow. Boxes are colored by kind (containers/aggregates teal, externals orange, contexts gold, commands yellow, events purple, views blue). Click to go deeper; leaf boxes jump to their section; use ◀ back to climb out.
